name: Puig del Far Loop
description: Hike the moderate 4.8-mile Puig del Far Loop for panoramic views of the Vilanova cliffs and dense forests in Espai Natural de les Guilleries
region_intro: Hiking around Vilanova De Sau offers diverse terrain characterized by the Sau Reservoir, the lush Guilleries Massif, and dramatic cliffs. The region features dense forests, elevated lookout points, and the unique partially submerged bell tower of Sant Romà de Sau. This landscape provides a variety of trails for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Vilanova De Sau The most popular hiking route is Puig del Far Loop, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the Sau Reservoir and surrounding massifs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tavertet Viewpoint – Puig de la Força loop from Mas la Riba, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This trail provides expansive views of the Tavertet cliffs and the Guilleries mountain range. Local hikers also love the Salt del Molí Bernat – View of the Cliffs of Tavertet loop from Campanar de Sant Romà de Sau, a 7.9 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the cliffs, often completed in about 4 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Vilanova De Sau is defined by the Sau Reservoir, the Guilleries Massif, and dramatic cliff formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels. The routes in Vilanova De Sau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 2500 hikers have used komoot to explore Vilanova De Sau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Vilanova de Sau?
A: Vilanova de Sau offers a diverse network of over 25 hiking routes. These trails cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, allowing you to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Vilanova de Sau?
A: The terrain around Vilanova de Sau is highly varied. You'll encounter paths winding through dense forests of the Guilleries Massif, dramatic cliff formations like the Cingles de les Balmes, and trails offering panoramic views of the Sau Reservoir. Some routes may include steep climbs, especially those leading from the lake, so sturdy footwear is recommended.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Vilanova de Sau has several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Romà de Sau – Spectacular View loop from Mas la Riba&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that allows you to enjoy the scenery without significant elevation changes. Overall, there are 9 easy routes available.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the most popular hikes in Vilanova de Sau are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981024383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig del Far Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5332555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de la Força loop from Campanar de Sant Romà de Sau&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy spectacular viewpoints such as Puig del Far and Roca del Migdia, offering expansive views of the Sau Reservoir, the Tavertet cliffs, and the Guilleries. The partially submerged bell tower of the 11th-century Romanesque church of Sant Romà de Sau is a unique and iconic sight, especially when water levels are low. You might also encounter impressive cliff formations and dense forests.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or water features accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region features several beautiful water features. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Molí Bernat&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21882878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Molí Bernat – View of the Cliffs of Tavertet loop from Campanar de Sant Romà de Sau&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/576239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sallent Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Waterfall and Mill&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I visit while hiking in Vilanova de Sau?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can explore the spectacular Romanesque complex of the Monastery of Sant Pere de Casserres, which sits on a peninsula overlooking the reservoir and is accessible by trail. The 11th-century Romanesque church of Sant Romà de Sau, often partially submerged, is another significant historical landmark.

Q: Is Vilanova de Sau suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Vilanova de Sau offers trails suitable for families. While some routes involve steep sections, there are easier, more accessible paths that allow families to enjoy the natural surroundings and unique sights like the Sau Reservoir. Always check the difficulty and length of a route to ensure it matches your family's abilities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Vilanova de Sau?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 390 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views, the unique sight of the submerged church, and the diverse landscapes that combine forests, cliffs, and the reservoir. The variety of trails for different skill levels is also a common highlight.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vilanova de Sau?
A: Vilanova de Sau is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter hikes are also possible, offering a different, often quieter, experience of the landscape.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Vilanova de Sau provides challenging routes for experienced hikers. While there are no routes explicitly graded as 'difficult' in our data, many moderate trails feature significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, especially those that climb from the lake up to the cliffs. These routes require good physical fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: What are the parking options for hikers in Vilanova de Sau?
A: Parking is generally available at popular trailheads and access points around the Sau Reservoir and nearby villages. Specific parking information can often be found in the details of individual komoot tours, so it's advisable to check the route description before you go.
tips: Puig del Far is a great place to see the Sau reservoir and on a clear day you can see the Pyrenees.
On one side of the road are the ruins of a romantic hermitage. There is a plaque explaining the history of the site. The site is interesting.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Puig del Far Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse terrain, including narrow, rugged mountain paths and some sections on asphalted tracks, especially when passing through villages and agricultural fields. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, with some parts of the ascent described as steep and rocky, requiring a good physical rating.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on the Puig del Far Loop?
faqs.answer: You can expect rich and varied scenery. A major highlight is the panoramic views of the distinctive reddish Vilanova cliffs (cingles de Vilanova) and the surrounding landscapes, particularly from the Puig del Far viewpoint. The route also winds through dense, lush forests of oaks, holm oaks, chestnut trees, and pines.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the primary viewpoint is Puig del Far, offering stunning panoramic views of the Vilanova cliffs. You'll also pass by the charming Ermita de Santa Margarida d'Ardol, a religious building with historical significance.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Puig del Far Loop trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in Vilanova de Sau. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking available within the village of Vilanova de Sau, which serves as a common starting point for local hikes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Puig del Far Loop?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this trail are generally spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant, with lush greenery in spring and beautiful colors in autumn. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially challenging conditions on steeper sections.

faqs.question: Is the Puig del Far Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona, it's essential to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Be mindful of steep and rocky sections that might be challenging for some dogs.

faqs.question: Is the Puig del Far Loop suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The Puig del Far Loop is graded as 'moderate' in difficulty. It includes steep and rocky ascents that require a good physical rating. While older, experienced children might manage, it's generally not recommended for beginners or very young children due to the significant elevation changes and challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike in the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Puig del Far Loop within the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona. However, always practice Leave No Trace principles and respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Does the Puig del Far Loop connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is also known as SL-C 122 and intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers 100% of the official 'Puig del Far' trail and significant portions of the 'Camí Oliba' and 'Folgueroles-Salt de la Minyona-Tavèrnoles' routes.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the area around the trail?
faqs.answer: The dense and rugged forests along the route were historically known as a haunt for old bandits ('bandolers'). Additionally, the distinctive reddish hue of the Vilanova cliffs inspired the nickname 'camaroigs' for the local inhabitants of Vilanova de Sau.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for hiking this loop?
faqs.answer: The ascent to Puig del Far involves a considerable incline and can be steep and rocky. It's advisable to wear sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support. Many sections of the trail lead to viewpoints only accessible on foot, so be prepared for some rewarding climbs.
